mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rate signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations. Lower cost of capital directly increases present value of corporate cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.33% as of June 2009 reflects post-crisis easing trajectory
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in < 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment signals labor market recovery and strengthening aggregate demand. Expanding employment base increases consumer spending capacity and corporate revenue expectations, driving positive equity returns over 60 trading days as earnings revisions propagate.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises > 0.5% month-over-month for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 157,232k as of June 2009 in post-recession recovery phase
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with labor market stabilization
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (mean > 0, t-test p > 0.05) in triggered periods, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth indicates consumer spending acceleration and demand-side momentum. Rising retail activity signals confidence and purchasing power, which translates to higher corporate revenues and equity valuations within 20 trading days as market reprices growth expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 705,692M as of May 2009 reflects early recovery demand
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in < 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth reflects capacity utilization recovery and manufacturing momentum. Rising production signals operational efficiency gains and pricing power, which compress cost structures and expand profit margins, producing negative returns only if production surge signals overheating; baseline expectation is positive 5-day returns as market reprices supply-side strength.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 102.67 as of May 2009 shows early recovery in manufacturing
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 5-day returns occur in < 52% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate decline combined with rising payroll employment signals robust labor market tightening. The joint signal of falling joblessness and job creation reduces recession risk premium and increases equity risk appetite, producing positive 20-day returns as investors rotate into cyclical equities.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls > 0.2pp month-over-month AND PAYEMS rises > 0.3% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.7% and PAYEMS at 157,232k as of June 2009
regime_hint: labor market recovery with dual confirmation
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in < 55% of jointly-triggered instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts combined with retail sales growth signals broad-based demand recovery across both residential and consumption channels. The joint expansion indicates sustained consumer confidence and construction activity, reducing recession probability and driving positive 60-day equity returns as cyclical sectors outperform.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises > 5% month-over-month AND RSAFS rises > 0.8% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,560k and RSAFS at 705,692M as of May 2009
regime_hint: synchronized demand recovery across housing and consumption
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in < 58% of jointly-triggered instances, or interaction term adds no predictive power (R² improvement < 0.01) over univariate models.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPI combined with low federal funds rate creates negative real rates that penalize cash holdings and incentivize equity allocation. The regime of accommodative monetary policy with rising price pressures drives portfolio rebalancing into equities, producing negative 20-day returns only if inflation spike triggers Fed tightening expectations; baseline is positive.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 308.85 and FEDFUNDS at 5.33% as of June 2009
regime_hint: negative real rates with inflation acceleration
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in < 52% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient reverses sign (becomes negative) in any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In post-recession recovery regimes, housing starts serve as a leading indicator of construction employment and consumer wealth effects. Rising housing activity predicts positive equity returns over 60 days as construction multipliers propagate through the economy and housing-sensitive sectors outperform. This mechanism is regime-specific and fails in late-cycle overheating.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,560k as of May 2009 reflects post-crisis housing recovery
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in < 55% of triggered instances during recovery regimes, or mechanism reverses (produces negative returns) when HOUST growth exceeds 15% month-over-month, indicating speculative overheating.
